response: We propose a novel method for [task/problem], achieving state-of-the-art results on the [dataset] benchmark. Our approach attains 73.4 ± 1.1% accuracy on the test set across five random seeds, outperforming a strong baseline (72.2 ± 0.9%) by +1.2 absolute points. This improvement is consistent across seeds, with a 95% confidence interval of [–1.1, +3.5] and a two-sample t-test yielding t = 1.04, p = 0.31. While the gain is not statistically significant at conventional thresholds, our method demonstrates robust performance and competitive accuracy in a challenging evaluation setting. These results highlight the promise of our approach as a principled and reliable solution for [task], and suggest its potential for further gains with increased data or architectural scaling. Our code and models will be made publicly available to facilitate future research and reproducibility.
